articles.title: Kyle Schwarber reveals tepid pursuit from Red Sox this past offseason
articles.description:
"[Boston’s pursuit] kind of just started and ended with the conversation.”

The post Kyle Schwarber reveals tepid pursuit from Red Sox this past offseason appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kyle Schwarber reported minimal interest from the Red Sox during the offseason.
Schwarber's discussions with Boston were brief and unproductive, lacking follow-up.
This tepid pursuit reflects the Red Sox's shifting focus in player acquisitions.

articles.title: 7 contracts Bruins might try to unload this offseason
articles.description:

summary: Bruins aim to unload contracts to increase their $16 million cap space.

Increased spending power may lead to key player acquisitions for Boston fans.

articles.title: First white shark sighting of 2026 confirmed off Massachusetts coast
articles.description:
A dead gray seal found on Martha’s Vineyard with suspected shark bite wounds led officials to confirm the season’s first white shark report in Massachusetts waters.

The post First white shark sighting of 2026 confirmed off Massachusetts coast appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: First white shark sighting of 2026 confirmed off Martha’s Vineyard.
A dead gray seal with shark bite wounds prompted the confirmation.
Residents should remain cautious during beach activities this season.

articles.title: Senate again rejects bid to end Iran war, but GOP opposition grows
articles.description:
Senator Lisa Murkowski of Alaska became the latest Republican to switch her vote to halt the conflict and require President Trump to win congressional approval to continue it.

The post Senate again rejects bid to end Iran war, but GOP opposition grows appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Senate rejects proposal to end Iran war, maintaining current military involvement.
Senator Lisa Murkowski joins GOP members opposing the conflict, increasing bipartisan dissent.
Continued military action may affect local Boston veterans and families concerned about war impacts.

articles.title: Senate confirms Trump pick Warsh as chairman of the Federal Reserve, following Powell
articles.description:
Kevin Warsh, 56, a former top Fed official, was confirmed Wednesday in a largely party-line 54-45 vote.

The post Senate confirms Trump pick Warsh as chairman of the Federal Reserve, following Powell appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chairman on May 13, 2026.
Warsh received a 54-45 vote, reflecting significant party division.
Boston residents may see changes in monetary policy affecting local economy.

articles.title: Prosecutors to retry Alex Murdaugh in deaths of wife and son after high court overturned convictions
articles.description:
Murdaugh, 57, will remain in prison. He pleaded guilty to stealing around $12 million from his clients and currently is serving a 40-year federal sentence.

The post Prosecutors to retry Alex Murdaugh in deaths of wife and son after high court overturned convictions appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Prosecutors will retry Alex Murdaugh for the 2021 murders of his wife and son.
Murdaugh, 57, is currently serving a 40-year federal sentence for stealing $12 million.
The retrial may affect perceptions of justice and safety in Boston's legal community.

articles.title: Want to own a piece of Dunkin’? Its parent company may go public.
articles.description:
If the plans move forward, it will be the third time that the coffee brand has gone public in the last eight decades.

summary: Dunkin's parent company plans to go public for the third time in 80 years.
Boston residents could invest in Dunkin's potential IPO, enhancing local economic engagement.
The IPO could influence Dunkin's local operations and community involvement in Boston.

articles.title: Trooper who helped take down Memorial Drive gunman identified as victims’ families speak out
articles.description:
Trooper Landon Veney’s actions “reflected the very best of law enforcement and public service under extraordinarily dangerous circumstances,” Gov. Maura Healey said.

The post Trooper who helped take down Memorial Drive gunman identified as victims’ families speak out appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Trooper Landon Veney helped apprehend a gunman on Memorial Drive in Cambridge.
Governor Maura Healey praised Veney's actions as exemplary of law enforcement on May 13, 2026.
Victims' families expressed gratitude for the swift response, enhancing community safety.
